copper.name: Copper
copper.sourcing_note: Host for Se, Te, Mo, Re, Au byproducts.
copper.uses: Conductors, windings, busbars, current collectors, chip interconnect, contacts.

aluminium.name: Aluminium
aluminium.sourcing_note: Bauxite → alumina → smelting; host for Ga byproduct.
aluminium.uses: ACSR conductors, foils, frames, heat sinks, casings, structure.

iron.name: Iron
iron.uses: Steel everywhere (towers, cores, rebar, vessels); iron-flow battery electrolyte.

prices_monthly.label: Global iron ore benchmark price (IMF, 62% Fe CFR China)

nickel.name: Nickel
nickel.uses: NMC/NCA cathodes, superalloys, SOFC, plating, alkaline electrolysers.

cobalt.name: Cobalt
cobalt.sourcing_note: Recovered from Cu (DRC) and Ni ores.
cobalt.uses: NMC/NCA cathodes, superalloy turbine blades, HDD media.

zinc.name: Zinc
zinc.sourcing_note: Host for In, Ge, Cd byproducts.

lead.name: Lead
lead.sourcing_note: Host for Bi byproduct; often mined with Zn.

titanium.name: Titanium
titanium.uses: Corrosion alloys, PEM fuel-cell plates, LTO anode; TiO₂ pigment feedstock.

chromium.name: Chromium
chromium.uses: Stainless / Cr-Mo steels, vacuum-interrupter contacts, photomask absorber.

molybdenum.name: Molybdenum
molybdenum.sourcing_note: Own mines plus byproduct of Cu porphyry.
molybdenum.uses: Arc contacts, EUV Mo/Si mirrors & masks, superalloys.

tungsten.name: Tungsten
tungsten.uses: Chip contacts/vias, arc contacts, superalloys, tooling.

vanadium.name: Vanadium
vanadium.sourcing_note: From titanomagnetite / steel slag; some primary.

tantalum.name: Tantalum
tantalum.sourcing_note: From tantalite / coltan; some Sn-slag co-product.
tantalum.uses: Chip barrier metal (Ta/TaN), MLCC & Ta capacitors.

hafnium.name: Hafnium
hafnium.sourcing_note: Separated from zirconium during Zr refining.
hafnium.uses: High-k gate dielectric (HfO₂), nuclear control rods, superalloys.
hafnium.prod_note: No per-country production data: hafnium is recovered only during zirconium refining — see zirconium for the host mineral supply.

zirconium.name: Zirconium
zirconium.sourcing_note: Zircon from Ti mineral sands; host for Hf.
zirconium.uses: Zircaloy fuel cladding, high-k precursor, LLZO solid electrolyte.

antimony.name: Antimony
antimony.uses: Lead-acid alloy, liquid-metal (Ca/Sb) battery, flame retardant, dopant.

silver.name: Silver
silver.sourcing_note: Recovered with Pb-Zn, Cu and Au ores.
silver.uses: Solar PV contacts, switch contacts, die-attach sinter, plating.

iridium.name: Iridium

trade_labels.711041: Iridium, osmium & ruthenium, unwrought or powder (shared code)

iridium.prod_note: No per-country production data: iridium is a minor co-product of PGM mining (South Africa dominant) — see platinum for the host supply.

ruthenium.name: Ruthenium
ruthenium.uses: HDD media underlayer, EUV mirror coatings, advanced-node interconnect.

trade_labels.711041: Iridium, osmium & ruthenium, unwrought or powder (shared code)

ruthenium.prod_note: No per-country production data: ruthenium is a minor co-product of PGM mining (South Africa dominant) — see platinum for the host supply.

rhenium.name: Rhenium
rhenium.sourcing_note: Recovered during molybdenite roasting (Mo often of Cu).

rare-earths.name: Rare earths (basket)
rare-earths.sourcing_note: Nd, Pr, Dy, Tb, Sm, Y, La, Ce, Er, Yb are co-products of the same ores (bastnäsite, monazite, ionic-adsorption clays); heavy REEs (Dy, Tb) are scarce co-products. No element-level country production data exists — the basket is tracked here.
rare-earths.uses: NdFeB magnets (Nd, Pr, Dy, Tb), SmCo magnets, YSZ & REBCO (Y), LLZO/SOFC (La), CMP slurry (Ce), fibre amplifiers (Er, Yb).

lithium.name: Lithium
lithium.uses: All Li-ion chemistries (cathode, salt); solid-state Li-metal.

sodium.name: Sodium
sodium.sourcing_note: From salt (NaCl) / brine / soda ash. Tracked via salt.
sodium.uses: Na-ion & NaS batteries, molten nitrate (CSP), chemicals.

potassium.name: Potassium
potassium.sourcing_note: From potash (sylvite / brine). Tracked via potash.

graphite.name: Graphite
graphite.sourcing_note: Natural flake ore, or synthetic from petroleum needle coke.

silicon.name: Silicon
silicon.sourcing_note: Quartz → metallurgical Si → polysilicon; chokepoint is purity/processing.
silicon.uses: Solar PV (polysilicon→wafer), semiconductor devices, Si anode, Fe-Si steel.

hp-quartz.name: High-purity quartz
hp-quartz.sourcing_note: Mined high-purity deposits (Spruce Pine, NC dominates crucible-grade supply).
hp-quartz.uses: Crucibles for Si crystal growth, optical-fibre feedstock, fused-silica masks/optics.
hp-quartz.prod_note: No per-country production data: USGS tracks only US high-purity quartz statistics; the crucible-grade supply is dominated by Spruce Pine (US).

phosphorus.name: Phosphorus / phosphate
phosphorus.uses: LFP/LMFP cathode, LiPF₆ salt, InP, dopant.

sulfur.name: Sulfur
sulfur.sourcing_note: Recovered from oil & gas desulfurisation and smelting.
sulfur.uses: Li-S (frontier), NaS battery, H₂SO₄ (lead-acid, fab clean, VRFB).

fluorine.name: Fluorine / fluorspar
fluorine.sourcing_note: Fluorspar (CaF₂); also fluorosilicic acid from phosphate.
fluorine.uses: LiPF₆, PVDF, PFSA membranes, SF₆, etch gases.

boron.name: Boron
boron.sourcing_note: Borate minerals (borax, colemanite); Turkiye + US dominate.
boron.uses: NdFeB magnets, neutron absorbers, fibreglass, amorphous metal, BF₃ dopant.

chlorine.name: Chlorine
chlorine.uses: Etch gases (Cl₂, BCl₃), ZEBRA NiCl₂ battery, chemicals.
chlorine.prod_note: No per-country production data: chlorine is made by chlor-alkali electrolysis wherever salt and power are available — see sodium (salt) for feedstock supply.

gallium.name: Gallium
gallium.sourcing_note: Recovered from bauxite/alumina (Bayer liquor); minor Zn.

germanium.name: Germanium
germanium.sourcing_note: Recovered from Zn smelting; coal fly ash.
germanium.uses: Fibre-optic core doping, IR optics, SiGe.
germanium.prod_note: No per-country production data: USGS withholds germanium output by country (company-proprietary); China dominates refined supply — see zinc for the host supply.

indium.name: Indium
indium.uses: ITO transparent conductors, CIGS solar, low-temp solder/TIM.

helium.name: Helium
helium.uses: HDD fill, semiconductor process & cooling, cryogenics (HTS, MRI).

trade_labels.280429: Rare gases other than argon (shared code)

neon.name: Neon
neon.sourcing_note: Cryogenic air separation; historically Ukraine/Russia purification.

trade_labels.280429: Rare gases other than argon (shared code)

neon.prod_note: USGS reports only US rare-gas output; global neon capacity is not tracked by country.

krypton-xenon.name: Krypton & xenon

trade_labels.280429: Rare gases other than argon (shared code)

krypton-xenon.prod_note: USGS reports only US rare-gas output; global Kr/Xe capacity is not tracked by country.

argon.name: Argon
argon.uses: Sputtering, inert atmosphere, welding, fab process.
argon.prod_note: No per-country production data: argon is separated from air wherever air-separation plants run; supply tracks industrial-gas capacity.

nitrogen.name: Nitrogen (fixed, as ammonia)
nitrogen.sourcing_note: Air separation; fixed nitrogen tracked via ammonia plants.
nitrogen.uses: Clean-air switchgear medium, NF₃ feedstock, process inerting; fertilisers.

711041.label: Iridium, osmium & ruthenium, unwrought or powder (shared code) (HS 711041)

280429.label: Rare gases other than argon (shared code) (HS 280429)
